generatedSentence: Jack, a grizzled mechanic, felt perpetually ticked off by the pervasive mayhem enacted by the shadowy Empress at the top of the city's stratified society. His old truck, a lifeline, often brought him past the city's grim lockups, where he sought answers for the constant injustice. His battered refrigerator held little more than an empanada, a stark contrast to the lavish lavenders and daffodil fields adorning the Empress's palace, a kinky display of wealth. He wished he were better armed, not just with a flimsy umbrella, but with real power to upgrade his life and recognize his own worth, a true garnet of hope, like a free goshawk. His odyssey began with a whispered call from a raffish aristocrat, a dealer of cryptic jackpots, whose offers often held a dark, underlying joke. Jack, a bit of a nutjob himself, was engaged to incorporate a new payload into an iceboat, a dangerous proposition on the shore. The job involved bypassing a manufacturer's tight security, a task that required him to hack into their system using a radio and a dashcam. He knew it was forbidden, but the negotiable fee was far and away beyond his minimum lifetime earnings. The potential for impulse decisions, like a mojito or brownie, would have to be set aside for the nighttime operation. The twosome, Jack and his unlikely partner, a nervous sailor who often spoke in a pantomime of languages, approached the target in their shiny iceboat. The facility, a massive structure of latticework and nubuck panels, was guarded by knights and a triton-like security force. Jack felt a dark impulse to jiggle the wires when he heard a low lullaby playing over the intercom, a strange form of psychological warfare. He found himself dialing a frequency, hoping to incorporate a kyanite-laced signal to madden the system, overriding the guards' patrol. The smell of hyssop and offal hung heavy, a premonition of the typhoon that was about to break. He voiced a holy plea as the system thwacked back, a jicama-like incisor of data slicing through his defenses. He felt a sudden, sharp hurt, a sense of rust spreading through his cybernetic tools. The guards, now alerted, moved like a goshawk, their octane-fueled speed uncommon. With a final surge of charity, he pushed an umbrella-shaped device, a desperate pantomime, to knock down their central server, clearing the path for their escape. As the alarms shrieked, the unwanted package, a bundle of certifications, was secured. The old man, once vilified, now looked at Jack with a glint of gratitude, the moonlight effervescing off his doeskin jacket, a silent whisper of a new beginning.
panelSentences: Jack, a grizzled mechanic, felt perpetually ticked off by the pervasive mayhem enacted by the shadowy Empress at the top of the city's stratified society. His old truck, a lifeline, often brought him past the city's grim lockups, where he sought answers for the constant injustice. His battered refrigerator held little more than an empanada, a stark contrast to the lavish lavenders and daffodil fields adorning the Empress's palace, a kinky display of wealth. He wished he were better armed, not just with a flimsy umbrella, but with real power to upgrade his life and recognize his own worth, a true garnet of hope, like a free goshawk.

His odyssey began with a whispered call from a raffish aristocrat, a dealer of cryptic jackpots, whose offers often held a dark, underlying joke. Jack, a bit of a nutjob himself, was engaged to incorporate a new payload into an iceboat, a dangerous proposition on the shore. The job involved bypassing a manufacturer's tight security, a task that required him to hack into their system using a radio and a dashcam. He knew it was forbidden, but the negotiable fee was far and away beyond his minimum lifetime earnings. The potential for impulse decisions, like a mojito or brownie, would have to be set aside for the nighttime operation.

The twosome, Jack and his unlikely partner, a nervous sailor who often spoke in a pantomime of languages, approached the target in their shiny iceboat. The facility, a massive structure of latticework and nubuck panels, was guarded by knights and a triton-like security force. Jack felt a dark impulse to jiggle the wires when he heard a low lullaby playing over the intercom, a strange form of psychological warfare. He found himself dialing a frequency, hoping to incorporate a kyanite-laced signal to madden the system, overriding the guards' patrol. The smell of hyssop and offal hung heavy, a premonition of the typhoon that was about to break.

He voiced a holy plea as the system thwacked back, a jicama-like incisor of data slicing through his defenses. He felt a sudden, sharp hurt, a sense of rust spreading through his cybernetic tools. The guards, now alerted, moved like a goshawk, their octane-fueled speed uncommon. With a final surge of charity, he pushed an umbrella-shaped device, a desperate pantomime, to knock down their central server, clearing the path for their escape. As the alarms shrieked, the unwanted package, a bundle of certifications, was secured. The old man, once vilified, now looked at Jack with a glint of gratitude, the moonlight effervescing off his doeskin jacket, a silent whisper of a new beginning.
rawPanelSentences: Jack, a grizzled mechanic, felt perpetually ticked off by the pervasive mayhem enacted by the shadowy Empress at the top of the city's stratified society. His old truck, a lifeline, often brought him past the city's grim lockups, where he sought answers for the constant injustice. His battered refrigerator held little more than an empanada, a stark contrast to the lavish lavenders and daffodil fields adorning the Empress's palace, a kinky display of wealth. He wished he were better armed, not just with a flimsy umbrella, but with real power to upgrade his life and recognize his own worth, a true garnet of hope, like a free goshawk.

His odyssey began with a whispered call from a raffish aristocrat, a dealer of cryptic jackpots, whose offers often held a dark, underlying joke. Jack, a bit of a nutjob himself, was engaged to incorporate a new payload into an iceboat, a dangerous proposition on the shore. The job involved bypassing a manufacturer's tight security, a task that required him to hack into their system using a radio and a dashcam. He knew it was forbidden, but the negotiable fee was far and away beyond his minimum lifetime earnings. The potential for impulse decisions, like a mojito or brownie, would have to be set aside for the nighttime operation.

The twosome, Jack and his unlikely partner, a nervous sailor who often spoke in a pantomime of languages, approached the target in their shiny iceboat. The facility, a massive structure of latticework and nubuck panels, was guarded by knights and a triton-like security force. Jack felt a dark impulse to jiggle the wires when he heard a low lullaby playing over the intercom, a strange form of psychological warfare. He found himself dialing a frequency, hoping to incorporate a kyanite-laced signal to madden the system, overriding the guards' patrol. The smell of hyssop and offal hung heavy, a premonition of the typhoon that was about to break.

He voiced a holy plea as the system thwacked back, a jicama-like incisor of data slicing through his defenses. He felt a sudden, sharp hurt, a sense of rust spreading through his cybernetic tools. The guards, now alerted, moved like a goshawk, their octane-fueled speed uncommon. With a final surge of charity, he pushed an umbrella-shaped device, a desperate pantomime, to knock down their central server, clearing the path for their escape. As the alarms shrieked, the unwanted package, a bundle of certifications, was secured. The old man, once vilified, now looked at Jack with a glint of gratitude, the moonlight effervescing off his doeskin jacket, a silent whisper of a new beginning.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a scruffy mechanic with a cybernetic arm and a worn leather jacket, a single glowing blue eye. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Jack, looking ticked and frustrated, sitting in his old truck, gazing up at a lavish, glowing palace in the distance, dramatic low-angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Jack and a nervous sailor in an advanced, shiny iceboat approaching a sprawling, futuristic facility made of latticework, moonlit, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Jack intensely focused, dialing a frequency on a complex radio device, while guards with triton-like helmets patrol a hyssop-filled corridor, close-up on Jack's determined face. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Jack, having just deployed a device, stands amidst sparking computer consoles, a large security panel knocked down, as alarms blare and guards rush towards him, dynamic action shot.

definitions.definition: Any of several birds of prey, principally in the genus Accipiter.
A hard transparent mineral that is often used as gemstones and abrasives.
A dark red color, like that of the gemstone.
Of a dark red colour.
A coarse mediaeval coat of defence, especially one made of leather.
A hard, fine-grained quartz that fractures conchoidally and generates sparks when struck.
A piece of flint, such as a gunflint, used to produce a spark by striking it with a firestriker.
A small cylinder of some other material of the same function in a cigarette lighter, etc.
A type of maize/corn with a hard outer hull.
Anything figuratively hard.
To furnish or decorate an object with flint.
A household appliance used for keeping food fresh by refrigeration (short form fridge).
One who has a chilling influence.
A state or situation of great confusion, disorder, trouble or destruction; chaos.
definitions.example: In all the mayhem, some children were separated from their partners.

definitions.definition: Infliction of violent injury on a person or thing.
definitions.example: The fighting dogs created mayhem in the flower beds.

definitions.definition: The maiming of a person by depriving him of the use of any of his limbs which are necessary for defense or protection.
The crime of damaging things or harming people on purpose.
Full of kinks; liable to kink or curl.
definitions.example: kinky hair

definitions.definition: Marked by unconventional sexual preferences or behavior, as fetishism, sadomasochism, and other sexual practices.
Queer; eccentric; crotchety.
The highest or uppermost part of something.
definitions.example: His kite got caught at the top of the tree.

definitions.definition: A child’s spinning toy; a spinning top.
definitions.example: The boy was amazed at how long the top would spin.

definitions.definition: (heading) Someone who is eminent.
(BDSM) A dominant partner in a BDSM relationship or roleplay.
A man penetrating or with a preference for penetrating during homosexual intercourse.
definitions.example: I prefer being a top, and my boyfriend prefers being a bottom.

definitions.definition: A top quark.
The utmost degree; the acme; the summit.
(ropemaking) A plug, or conical block of wood, with longitudinal grooves on its surface, in which the strands of the rope slide in the process of twisting.
(sound) Highest pitch or loudest volume.
definitions.example: She sang at the top of her voice.

definitions.definition: (wool manufacture) A bundle or ball of slivers of combed wool, from which the noils, or dust, have been taken out.
(obsolete, except in one sense of phrase on top of) Eve; verge; point.
The part of a cut gem between the girdle, or circumference, and the table, or flat upper surface.
(in the plural) Topboots.
A stroke on the top of the ball.
A forward spin given to the ball by hitting it on or near the top.
(in restaurants, preceded by a number) (A table at which there is, or which has enough seats for) a group of a specified number of people eating at a restaurant.
To cover on the top or with a top.
definitions.example: I like my ice cream topped with chocolate sauce.

definitions.definition: To cut or remove the top (as of a tree)
definitions.example: I don't want to be bald, so just top my hair.

definitions.definition: To excel, to surpass, to beat.
definitions.example: Titanic was the most successful film ever until it was topped by another Cameron film, Avatar.

definitions.definition: To be in the lead, to be at number one position (of).
definitions.example: Celine Dion topped the UK music charts twice in the 1990s.

definitions.definition: To commit suicide.
definitions.example: Depression causes many people to top themselves.

definitions.definition: To murder.
(BDSM) To be the dominant partner in a BDSM relationship or roleplay.
definitions.example: Giving advice to the dominant partner on how to run the BDSM session is called "topping from the bottom".

definitions.definition: (gay sexuality) To be the partner who penetrates in anal sex.
(gay sexuality) To anally penetrate.
To rise aloft; to be eminent; to tower.
definitions.example: lofty ridges and topping mountains

definitions.definition: To predominate.
definitions.example: topping passions

definitions.definition: To excel; to rise above others.
To raise one end of (a yard, etc.), making it higher than the other.
(dyeing) To cover with another dye.
definitions.example: to top aniline black with methyl violet to prevent greening

definitions.definition: To put a stiffening piece or back on (a saw blade).
To arrange (fruit, etc.) with the best on top.
(of a horse) To strike the top of (an obstacle) with the hind feet while jumping, so as to gain new impetus.
To improve (domestic animals, especially sheep) by crossing certain individuals or breeds with other superior breeds.
To cut, break, or otherwise take off the top of (a steel ingot) to remove unsound metal.
To strike (the ball) above the centre; also, to make (a stroke, etc.) by hitting the ball in this way.
Situated on the top of something.
Best; of the highest quality or rank.

definitions.definition: Very good, of high quality, power, or rank.
definitions.example: He's a top lawyer.

definitions.definition: Rated first.

definitions.definition: To make a clicking noise similar to the movement of the hands in an analog clock.
To make a tick or checkmark.
To work or operate, especially mechanically.

definitions.definition: To strike gently; to pat.
To add a bird to a list of birds that have been seen (or heard).
To go on trust, or credit.
To give tick; to trust.
Ticked off; annoyed.
A small wheel or roller, specifically the wheel of a gun carriage.
The ball on top of a flagpole.

On a wooden mast, a circular disc (or sometimes a rectangle) of wood near or at the top of the mast, usually with holes or sheaves to reeve signal halyards; also a temporary or emergency place for a lookout. "Main" refers to the mainmast, whereas a truck on another mast may be called (on the mizzenmast, for example) "mizzen-truck".
A semi-tractor ("semi") trailer; a lorry.

definitions.definition: Any motor vehicle designed for carrying cargo, including delivery vans, pickups, and other motorized vehicles (including passenger autos) fitted with a bed designed to carry goods.

A pantechnicon (removal van).
A flatbed railway car; a flatcar.

A pivoting frame, one attached to the bottom of the bed of a railway car at each end, that rests on the axle and which swivels to allow the axle (at each end of which is a solid wheel) to turn with curves in the track; a bogie.
The part of a skateboard or roller skate that joins the wheels to the deck, consisting of a hanger, baseplate, kingpin, and bushings, and sometimes mounted with a riser in between.

Dirt or other messiness.

definitions.definition: The root cause or main issue, especially an unexpected one
A laughably worthless thing or person; a sham.

definitions.definition: To do or say something for amusement rather than seriously.

definitions.definition: (intransitive, followed by with) To dupe in a friendly manner for amusement; to mess with, play with.

definitions.definition: To make merry with; to make jokes upon; to rally.

definitions.definition: Any of a variety of stuffed pastries found in Spanish and Latin American cuisine.
To chop or cut down in a rough manner.

definitions.definition: To cough noisily.

definitions.definition: To withstand or put up with a difficult situation.

definitions.definition: To make a quick code change to patch a computer program, often one that, while being effective, is inelegant or makes the program harder to maintain.
definitions.example: I hacked in a fix for this bug, but we'll still have to do a real fix later.

definitions.definition: To accomplish a difficult programming task.
definitions.example: He can hack like no one else and make the program work as expected.

definitions.definition: To work with something on an intimately technical level.
definitions.example: I'm currently hacking distributed garbage collection.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To apply a trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to something to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.
definitions.example: I read up on dating tips so I can hack my sex life.

definitions.definition: To hack into; to gain unauthorized access to (a computer system, e.g., a website, or network) by manipulating code.
(by extension) To gain unauthorised access to a computer or online account belonging to (a person or organisation).
definitions.example: When I logged into the social network, I discovered I'd been hacked.

definitions.definition: To strike an opponent's leg with one's hockey stick.
definitions.example: He's going to the penalty box after hacking the defender in front of the goal.

definitions.definition: To make a flailing attempt to hit the puck with a hockey stick.
definitions.example: There's a scramble in front of the net as the forwards are hacking at the bouncing puck.

definitions.definition: To swing at a pitched ball.

To strike in a frantic movement.
To strike lightly as part of tapotement massage.
To lay (bricks) on a rack to dry.
To keep (young hawks) in a state of partial freedom, before they are trained.
To make common or cliched; to vulgarise.
To ride a horse at a regular pace; to ride on a road (as opposed to riding cross-country etc.).

To use frequently and indiscriminately, so as to render trite and commonplace.

Characterized by careless unconventionality; rakish.

A by-product of the grain milling process, which may include bran, husks, etc.
A dead body; carrion.
That which is thrown away as worthless or unfit for use; refuse; rubbish.
A proposal that has been made.

definitions.definition: Something put forth, bid, proffered or tendered.
definitions.example: His offer was $3.50 per share.

definitions.definition: An invitation to enter into a binding contract communicated to another party which contains terms sufficiently definite to create an enforceable contract if the other party accepts the invitation.
definitions.example: His first letter was not a real offer, but an attempt to determine interest.

definitions.definition: To propose or express one's willingness (to do something).
definitions.example: She offered to help with her homework.

definitions.definition: To present in words; to proffer; to make a proposal of; to suggest.
definitions.example: Everybody offered an opinion.

definitions.definition: To place at someone’s disposal; to present (something) to be either accepted or turned down.
definitions.example: He offered use of his car for the week.  He offered his good will for the Councilman's vote.

definitions.definition: To present (something) to God or gods as a gesture of worship, or for a sacrifice.
To place (something) in a position where it can be added to an existing mechanical assembly.
To bid, as a price, reward, or wages.
definitions.example: I offered twenty dollars for it. The company is offering a salary of £30,000 a year.

definitions.definition: To happen, to present itself.

Being or constituting a pair; two.
Twofold; double.
Performed by two individuals.

definitions.definition: A thrust; a push; a sudden force that impels.

definitions.example: The impulse to learn drove me to study night and day.

definitions.definition: The integral of force over time.
definitions.example: The total impulse from the impact will depend on the kinetic energy of the bullet.

definitions.definition: To impel; to incite.
To live or reside in.
definitions.example: The Inuit inhabit the Arctic.

definitions.definition: To be present in; to occupy.
definitions.example: Strange thoughts inhabit my mind.

definitions.definition: A digital video recorder mounted on the dashboard of a vehicle or elsewhere inside the vehicle to record occurrences in the vicinity, such as traffic accidents that the vehicle has been involved in, to provide evidence for criminal prosecutions, insurance claims, etc.
A person who makes jokes.
A funny person.
A jester.
A playing card that features a picture of a joker (that is, a jester) and that may be used as a wild card in some card games.
An unspecified, vaguely disreputable person.

definitions.definition: A man.
A clause in a contract that undermines its apparent provisions.

definitions.definition: To utter audibly, with tone and not just breath.

definitions.example: voice the pipes of an organ

Sounded with vibration of the vocal cords. For example, the phone [z] is voiced, while [s] is unvoiced.

definitions.definition: (of a signal) That contains voice.
A thing that is extremely holy; used almost exclusively in Holy of Holies.

definitions.definition: Revered in a religion.
Morally perfect or flawless, or nearly so.

Any of several similar plants
The sagebrush (Artemisia spp.)
A plant used used for sprinkling of blood or water in purification rituals and at Passover, Origanum syriacum
A young servant or follower; a trained military attendant in service of a lord.

definitions.definition: (modern) A person on whom a knighthood has been conferred by a monarch.
A brave, chivalrous and honorable man devoted to a noble cause or love interest.
A chess piece, often in the shape of a horse's head, that is moved two squares in one direction and one at right angles to that direction in a single move, leaping over any intervening pieces.
A playing card bearing the figure of a knight; the knave or jack.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Ypthima.

A thin board on which a painter lays and mixes colours.
The range of colors in a given work or item or body of work.
A visual selection of colours, tools, commands, etc.
A plate of armour covering the points of junction at the bend of the shoulders and elbows.
A plate against which a person presses their breast to give force to a hand-operated drill.
That part of a cargo that produces revenue.
The total weight of passengers, crew, equipment and cargo carried by an aircraft or spacecraft.
That part of a rocket, missile, propelled stinger or torpedo that is not concerned with propulsion or guidance, such as a warhead or satellite.
The functional part of a computer virus or another type of malware program, rather than the part that spreads it.
(communication) The actual data in a data stream.
Rare; not readily found; unusual.

definitions.definition: Remarkable; exceptional.

definitions.definition: Exceedingly, exceptionally.

A weather phenomenon in the northwestern Pacific that is precisely equivalent to a hurricane, which results in wind speeds of 64 knots (118 km/h) or above. Equivalent to a cyclone in the Indian Ocean and Indonesia/Australia.
To swirl like a hurricane.
A cradlesong, a soothing song to calm children or lull them to sleep.
To sing a lullaby to.
To include (something) as a part.

definitions.definition: To mix (something in) as an ingredient; to blend

definitions.definition: To admit as a member of a company
To form into a legal company.

definitions.definition: To include (another clause or guarantee of the US constitution) as a part (of the Fourteenth Amendment, such that the clause binds not only the federal government but also state governments).
To form into a body; to combine, as different ingredients, into one consistent mass.
To unite with a material body; to give a material form to; to embody.
Corporate; incorporated; made one body, or united in one body; associated; mixed together; combined; embodied.
Not consisting of matter; not having a material body; incorporeal; spiritual.

Someone knowledgeable in the practical management of ships.

Anything shiny; a trinket.

Excellent; remarkable.

Having hidden meaning.

definitions.definition: To sport, engage in harmless pranking.
To frolic, engage in carefree adventure.
A spirited or playful adventure.

(usually groceries) retail foodstuffs and other household supplies.

definitions.definition: The furthest limit, as to number, quantity, extent, etc.

definitions.definition: The side of a curved road, racetrack etc. that has the longer arc length; the side of a racetrack furthest from the interior of the course or some other point of reference.
definitions.example: On the final bend, the second-place car tried to go around the outside of the leader but spun off into the barrier.

definitions.definition: A passenger riding on the outside of a coach or carriage.
Of or pertaining to the outer surface, limit or boundary.

definitions.definition: Of, pertaining to or originating from beyond the outer surface, limit or boundary.
Away from the interior or center of something.
Originating from, arranged by, or being someone outside an organization, group, etc.

definitions.definition: Extending or going beyond the borders or scope of an organization, group, etc.
(of a pitch) Away (far) from the batter as it crosses home plate.

definitions.definition: Reaching the extreme or farthest limit, as to extent, quantity, etc; maximum.

definitions.definition: Positioned towards the central division of a road: towards the right-hand side if one drives on the left, or left-hand side if one drives on the right.

To or in the outdoors or outside; to or in an area that is beyond the scope, limits or borders of a given place.

definitions.definition: On the outside of, not inside (something, such as a building).

definitions.definition: Near, but not in.
(usually with “of”) Except, apart from.

definitions.definition: To remove obstructions, impediments or other unwanted items from.

definitions.definition: To become free from obstruction or obscurement; to become transparent.

definitions.definition: To eliminate ambiguity or doubt from (a matter); to clarify or resolve; to clear up.

definitions.definition: To remove from suspicion, especially of having committed a crime.

definitions.definition: To pass without interference; to miss.

definitions.definition: To finish or complete (a stage, challenge, or game).

definitions.definition: Of a check or financial transaction, to go through as payment; to be processed so that the money is transferred.

definitions.definition: To earn a profit of; to net.

definitions.definition: To approve or authorise for a particular purpose or action; to give clearance to.

definitions.definition: To obtain approval or authorisation in respect of.

definitions.definition: To obtain a clearance.

To hit, kick, head, punch etc. (a ball, puck) away in order to defend one's goal.

definitions.definition: To demolish.
